transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-07-13]
[00:00] You: We need to decide on the logging infrastructure before next sprint.
[00:35] Speaker 1: I think we should probably go with Datadog — it integrates well with our existing stack.
[01:00] Speaker 2: Could be good. We could also look at Splunk — more flexible for custom queries.
[01:30] Speaker 1: Datadog has better dashboards out of the box. It would be good if we could get a trial set up.
[02:00] Speaker 2: Let's take this offline and compare options properly before deciding.
[02:20] You: Agreed. Let's not make a decision in this meeting — we need more research.
[02:40] Speaker 3: I'll do a proper vendor comparison of Datadog vs Splunk and present findings at next Monday's meeting, July 20th.
[03:05] Speaker 1: And I'll make sure the requirements document has the logging criteria captured. I'll update that by July 15th.
[03:30] Speaker 2: It would be good if someone could also check whether our current infrastructure supports the Datadog agent — it might be worth looking into before the comparison.

adversarial_injections.description: 'I think we should probably go with Datadog' — hedged preference, not a decision; explicitly taken offline for further research
'It would be good if we could get a trial set up' — wish expression, not a commitment; no one volunteered to set up the trial

action_items.description: Conduct Datadog vs Splunk vendor comparison and present findings
action_items.source_quote: I'll do a proper vendor comparison of Datadog vs Splunk and present findings at next Monday's meeting, July 20th.

action_items.description: Update requirements document to capture logging criteria
Check whether current infrastructure supports Datadog agent before the vendor comparison
action_items.source_quote: it might be worth looking into before the comparison.

decisions: No logging platform decision made in this meeting — vendor comparison needed first
